tEn: The Curious Case of Neural Text Degeneration
tZh: 神经文本退化的奇特案例
sEn: Decoding strategies significantly affect machine text quality, and Nucleus Sampling improves diversity and fluency.
sZh: 解码策略显著影响机器文本质量，核采样方法能提升多样性与流畅性。
absEn: Despite considerable advancements with deep neural language models, the enigma of neural text degeneration persists when these models are tested as text generators. The counter-intuitive empirical observation is that even though the use of likelihood as training objective leads to high quality models for a broad range of language understanding tasks, using likelihood as a decoding objective leads to text that is bland and strangely repetitive. In this paper, we reveal surprising distributional differences between human text and machine text. In addition, we find that decoding strategies alone can dramatically effect the quality of machine text, even when generated from exactly the same neural language model. Our findings motivate Nucleus Sampling, a simple but effective method to draw the best out of neural generation. By sampling text from the dynamic nucleus of the probability distribution, which allows for diversity while effectively truncating the less reliable tail of the distribution, the resulting text better demonstrates the quality of human text, yielding enhanced diversity without sacrificing fluency and coherence.
absZh: 尽管深度神经语言模型取得了显著进展，但当这些模型作为文本生成器进行测试时，神经文本退化的谜团依然存在。反直觉的经验观察是，虽然使用似然作为训练目标可以为广泛的语言理解任务带来高质量模型，但将似然作为解码目标会导致文本平淡且奇怪地重复。在本文中，我们揭示了人类文本与机器文本之间令人惊讶的分布差异。此外，我们发现即使使用完全相同的神经语言模型，解码策略本身也能显著影响机器文本的质量。我们的发现推动了核采样（Nucleus Sampling）的发展，这是一种简单而有效的方法，能够充分发挥神经生成的潜力。通过从概率分布的动态核中采样文本，该方法在允许多样性的同时有效截断了分布中不太可靠的尾部，生成的文本更好地展现了人类文本的质量，在保持流畅性和连贯性的同时增强了多样性。

contrib.en: Reveals surprising distributional differences between human and machine text.
contrib.zh: 揭示了人类文本与机器文本之间令人惊讶的分布差异。

contrib.en: Finds that decoding strategies alone dramatically affect machine text quality.
contrib.zh: 发现仅解码策略就能显著影响机器文本质量。

contrib.en: Proposes Nucleus Sampling, a simple and effective decoding method.
contrib.zh: 提出Nucleus Sampling，一种简单有效的解码方法。

contrib.en: Shows maximization-based decoding leads to bland, repetitive text.
contrib.zh: 表明基于最大化的解码导致文本平淡且重复。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that Nucleus Sampling yields diverse and coherent text.
contrib.zh: 证明Nucleus Sampling生成多样且连贯的文本。

contrib.en: Uses HUSE evaluation to show Nucleus Sampling as the best decoding strategy.
contrib.zh: 使用HUSE评估表明Nucleus Sampling是最佳解码策略。

limits.en: Focuses on open-ended generation, not directed tasks like translation.
limits.zh: 专注于开放式生成，而非翻译等定向任务。

limits.en: Experiments limited to GPT-2 Large model; may not generalize.
limits.zh: 实验仅限于GPT-2 Large模型，可能不具普适性。

limits.en: Nucleus Sampling requires tuning threshold p for optimal performance.
limits.zh: Nucleus Sampling需要调整阈值p以获得最佳性能。

limits.en: Does not address training-time solutions for text degeneration.
limits.zh: 未解决训练阶段的文本退化问题。

limits.en: Human evaluation limited to 200 generations per method.
limits.zh: 每种方法的人类评估仅限200个生成样本。

items.en: Despite considerable advances in neural language modeling, it remains an open question what the best decoding strategy is for text generation from a language model (e.g. to generate a story). The counter-intuitive empirical observation is that even though the use of likelihood as training objective leads to high quality models for a broad range of language understanding tasks, maximization-based decoding methods such as beam search lead to degeneration — output text that is bland, incoherent, or gets stuck in repetitive loops.
items.zh: 尽管神经语言建模取得了显著进展，但从语言模型生成文本（例如生成故事）的最佳解码策略仍是一个未解问题。反直觉的经验观察是，尽管使用似然作为训练目标能为广泛的语言理解任务产生高质量模型，但基于最大化的解码方法（如束搜索）会导致退化——输出文本平淡、不连贯或陷入重复循环。

items.en: To address this we propose Nucleus Sampling, a simple but effective method to draw considerably higher quality text out of neural language models than previous decoding strategies. Our approach avoids text degeneration by truncating the unreliable tail of the probability distribution, sampling from the dynamic nucleus of tokens containing the vast majority of the probability mass.
items.zh: 为解决这一问题，我们提出核采样（Nucleus Sampling），一种简单而有效的方法，能从神经语言模型中生成比先前解码策略质量高得多的文本。我们的方法通过截断概率分布中不可靠的尾部，从包含绝大多数概率质量的动态核中采样，从而避免文本退化。

items.en: To properly examine current maximization-based and stochastic decoding methods, we compare generations from each of these methods to the distribution of human text along several axes such as likelihood, diversity, and repetition. Our results show that (1) maximization is an inappropriate decoding objective for open-ended text generation, (2) the probability distributions of the best current language models have an unreliable tail which needs to be truncated during generation and (3) Nucleus Sampling is currently the best available decoding strategy for generating long-form text that is both high-quality — as measured by human evaluation — and as diverse as human-written text.
items.zh: 为了恰当检验当前基于最大化和随机解码方法，我们将每种方法生成的文本与人类文本在似然、多样性和重复性等多个维度上进行对比。我们的结果表明：(1) 最大化对于开放式文本生成是不合适的解码目标，(2) 当前最佳语言模型的概率分布存在不可靠的尾部，需要在生成过程中截断，(3) 核采样是目前生成高质量（根据人工评估）且与人类书写文本一样多样化的长文本的最佳可用解码策略。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Even with substantial human context and the powerful GPT-2 Large language model, Beam Search (size 32) leads to degenerate repetition (highlighted in blue) while pure sampling leads to incoherent gibberish (highlighted in red). When b≥64𝑏64b\geq 64, both GPT-2 Large and XL (774M and 1542M parameters, respectively) prefer to stop generating immediately after the given context.
items.capZh: 图1：即使有大量人类上下文和强大的GPT-2 Large语言模型，束搜索（束宽32）也会导致退化性重复（蓝色高亮），而纯采样则导致不连贯的胡言乱语（红色高亮）。当b≥64时，GPT-2 Large和XL（分别为774M和1542M参数）都倾向于在给定上下文后立即停止生成。

items.en: On February 14th 2019, OpenAI surprised the scientific community with an impressively high-quality article about Ovid’s Unicorn, written by GPT-2.111https://openai.com/blog/better-language-models/ Notably, the top-quality generations obtained from the model rely on randomness in the decoding method, in particular through top-k𝑘k sampling that samples the next word from the top k𝑘k most probable choices (Fan et al., 2018; Holtzman et al., 2018; Radford et al., 2019), instead of aiming to decode text that maximizes likelihood.
items.zh: 2019年2月14日，OpenAI用一篇关于奥维德独角兽的令人印象深刻的高质量文章震惊了科学界，该文章由GPT-2生成。值得注意的是，从模型中获得的高质量生成依赖于解码方法中的随机性，特别是通过top-k采样，即从前k个最可能的候选中采样下一个词，而不是旨在解码最大化似然的文本。

items.en: In fact, decoding strategies that optimize for output with high probability, such as beam search, lead to text that is incredibly degenerate, even when using state-of-the-art models such as GPT-2 Large, as shown in Figure 1. This may seem counter-intuitive, as one would expect that good models would assign higher probability to more human-like, grammatical text. Indeed, language models do generally assign high scores to well-formed text, yet the highest scores for longer texts are often generic, repetitive, and awkward. Figure 2 exposes how different the distribution of probabilities assigned to beam search decoded text and naturally occurring text really are.
items.zh: 事实上，优化高概率输出的解码策略（如束搜索）会导致极其退化的文本，即使使用最先进的模型（如GPT-2 Large）也是如此，如图1所示。这似乎反直觉，因为人们会期望好的模型将更高的概率分配给更像人类、语法正确的文本。确实，语言模型通常对格式良好的文本赋予高分，但较长文本的最高分往往是通用、重复和别扭的。图2揭示了束搜索解码文本和自然文本的概率分布差异有多大。

items.en: Perhaps equally surprising is the right side of Figure 1, which shows that pure sampling — sampling directly from the probabilities predicted by the model — results in text that is incoherent and almost unrelated to the context. Why is text produced by pure sampling so degenerate? In this work we show that the “unreliable tail” is to blame. This unreliable tail is composed of tens of thousands of candidate tokens with relatively low probability that are over-represented in the aggregate.
items.zh: 也许同样令人惊讶的是图1的右侧，它显示纯采样——直接从模型预测的概率中采样——产生的文本不连贯且几乎与上下文无关。为什么纯采样产生的文本如此退化？在这项工作中，我们表明“不可靠的尾部”是罪魁祸首。这个不可靠的尾部由数万个概率相对较低的候选词组成，这些词在总体上被过度代表。

items.en: To overcome these issues we introduce Nucleus Sampling (§3.1). The key intuition of Nucleus Sampling is that the vast majority of probability mass at each time step is concentrated in the nucleus, a small subset of the vocabulary that tends to range between one and a thousand candidates. Instead of relying on a fixed top-k𝑘k, or using a temperature parameter to control the shape of the distribution without sufficiently suppressing the unreliable tail, we propose sampling from the top-p𝑝p portion of the probability mass, expanding and contracting the candidate pool dynamically.
items.zh: 为了克服这些问题，我们引入了核采样（§3.1）。核采样的关键直觉是，每个时间步的绝大多数概率质量集中在核中，即词汇表的一个小子集，通常范围在1到1000个候选词之间。我们不依赖固定的top-k，也不使用温度参数来控制分布形状而不充分抑制不可靠的尾部，而是建议从概率质量的前p部分采样，动态扩展和收缩候选池。

items.en: In order to compare current methods to Nucleus Sampling, we compare various distributional properties of generated text to the reference distribution, such as the likelihood of veering into repetition and the perplexity of generated text.
items.zh: 为了比较当前方法与核采样，我们将生成文本的各种分布属性与参考分布进行比较，例如陷入重复的似然和生成文本的困惑度。

items.capEn: Figure 2: The probability assigned to tokens generated by Beam Search and humans, given the same context. Note the increased variance that characterizes human text, in contrast with the endless repetition of text decoded by Beam Search.
items.capZh: 图2：在相同上下文下，束搜索和人类生成的词被分配的概率。注意人类文本的特征是方差增加，而束搜索解码的文本则是无休止的重复。

items.en: The latter reveals that text generated by maximization or top-k𝑘k sampling is too probable, indicating a lack of diversity and divergence in vocabulary usage from the human distribution. On the other hand, pure sampling produces text that is significantly less likely than the gold, corresponding to lower generation quality.
items.zh: 后者揭示，通过最大化或top-k采样生成的文本概率过高，表明缺乏多样性且词汇使用与人类分布存在差异。另一方面，纯采样产生的文本概率显著低于黄金标准，对应较低的生成质量。

items.en: Vocabulary usage and Self-BLEU (Zhu et al., 2018) statistics reveal that high values of k𝑘k are needed to make top-k𝑘k sampling match human statistics. Yet, generations based on high values of k𝑘k often have high variance in likelihood, hinting at qualitatively observable incoherency issues. Nucleus Sampling can easily match reference perplexity through tuning the value of p𝑝p, avoiding the incoherence caused by setting k𝑘k high enough to match distributional statistics.
items.zh: 词汇使用和Self-BLEU统计显示，需要较高的k值才能使top-k采样匹配人类统计量。然而，基于高k值的生成通常具有高似然方差，暗示了可定性观察到的不连贯问题。核采样可以通过调整p值轻松匹配参考困惑度，避免了因设置足够高的k以匹配分布统计量而导致的不连贯。

items.en: Finally, we perform Human Unified with Statistical Evaluation (HUSE; Hashimoto et al., 2019) to jointly assess the overall quality and diversity of the decoding strategies, which cannot be captured using either human or automatic evaluation alone. The HUSE evaluation demonstrates that Nucleus Sampling is the best overall decoding strategy. We include generated examples for qualitative analysis – see Figure 3 for a representative example, and further examples in the appendix.222Code and all generations are available at https://github.com/ari-holtzman/degen
items.zh: 最后，我们进行人类统一统计评估（HUSE），以联合评估解码策略的整体质量和多样性，这无法单独通过人工或自动评估捕捉。HUSE评估表明核采样是最佳的整体解码策略。我们提供了生成示例用于定性分析——见图3的代表性示例，以及附录中的更多示例。代码和所有生成结果可在https://github.com/ari-holtzman/degen获取。

items.en: A number of recent works have alluded to the disadvantages of generation by maximization, which tend to generate output with high grammaticality but low diversity (Kulikov et al., 2019; Holtzman et al., 2018; Fan et al., 2018). Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) have been a prominent research direction (Yu et al., 2017; Xu et al., 2018), but recent work has shown that when quality and diversity are considered jointly, GAN-generated text fails to outperform generations from language models (Caccia et al., 2018; Tevet et al., 2019; Semeniuta et al., 2018). Work on neural dialog systems have proposed methods for diverse beam search, using a task-specific diversity scoring function or constraining beam hypotheses to be sufficiently different (Li et al., 2016a; Vijayakumar et al., 2018; Kulikov et al., 2019; Pal et al., 2006). While such utility functions encourage desirable properties in generations, they do not remove the need to choose an appropriate decoding strategy, and we believe that Nucleus Sampling will have complementary advantages in such approaches. Finally, Welleck et al. (2020) begin to address the problem of neural text degeneration through an “unlikelihood loss”, which decreases training loss on repeated tokens and thus implicitly reduces gradients on frequent tokens as well. Our focus is on exposing neural text degeneration and providing a decoding solution that can be used with arbitrary models, but future work will likely combine training-time and inference-time solutions.
items.zh: 最近的一些工作提到了最大化生成的缺点，即倾向于生成语法正确但多样性低的输出。生成对抗网络（GAN）是一个突出的研究方向，但最近的工作表明，当同时考虑质量和多样性时，GAN生成的文本未能超越语言模型的生成。关于神经对话系统的工作提出了多样化束搜索的方法，使用任务特定的多样性评分函数或约束束假设足够不同。虽然这些效用函数鼓励生成中的理想属性，但它们并未消除选择适当解码策略的需求，我们相信核采样将在这些方法中具有互补优势。最后，Welleck等人开始通过“非似然损失”解决神经文本退化问题，该损失降低了重复词上的训练损失，从而也隐式减少了频繁词上的梯度。我们的重点是揭示神经文本退化并提供可与任意模型一起使用的解码解决方案，但未来的工作可能会结合训练时和推理时的解决方案。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Example generations continuing an initial sentence. Maximization and top-k𝑘k truncation methods lead to copious repetition (highlighted in blue), while sampling with and without temperature tends to lead to incoherence (highlighted in red). Nucleus Sampling largely avoids both issues.
items.capZh: 图3：延续初始句子的示例生成。最大化和top-k截断方法导致大量重复（蓝色高亮），而带温度和不带温度的采样往往导致不连贯（红色高亮）。核采样在很大程度上避免了这两个问题。

items.en: Many text generation tasks are defined through (input, output) pairs, such that the output is a constrained transformation of the input. Example applications include machine translation (Bahdanau et al., 2015), data-to-text generation (Wiseman et al., 2017), and summarization (Nallapati et al., 2016). We refer to these tasks as directed generation. Typically encoder-decoder architectures are used, often with an attention mechanism (Bahdanau et al., 2015; Luong et al., 2015) or using attention-based architectures such as the Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017). Generation is usually performed using beam search; since output is tightly scoped by the input, repetition and genericness are not as problematic. Still, similar issues have been reported when using large beam sizes (Koehn & Knowles, 2017) and more recently with exact inference (Stahlberg & Byrne, 2019), a counter-intuitive observation since more comprehensive search helps maximize probability.
items.zh: 许多文本生成任务通过（输入，输出）对定义，使得输出是输入的受限变换。示例应用包括机器翻译、数据到文本生成和摘要。我们将这些任务称为定向生成。通常使用编码器-解码器架构，通常带有注意力机制或使用基于注意力的架构如Transformer。生成通常使用束搜索进行；由于输出受输入严格限制，重复和通用性问题不那么严重。尽管如此，使用大束宽时也报告了类似问题，最近还有精确推理的问题，这是一个反直觉的观察，因为更全面的搜索有助于最大化概率。

items.en: Open-ended generation, which includes conditional story generation and contextual text continuation (as in Figure 1), has recently become a promising research direction due to significant advances in neural language models (Clark et al., 2018; Holtzman et al., 2018; Fan et al., 2018; Peng et al., 2018; Radford et al., 2019). While the input context restricts the space of acceptable output generations, there is a considerable degree of freedom in what can plausibly come next, unlike in directed generation settings. Our work addresses the challenges faced by neural text generation with this increased level of freedom, but we note that some tasks, such as goal-oriented dialog, may fall somewhere in between open-ended and directed generation.
items.zh: 开放式生成，包括条件故事生成和上下文文本延续（如图1所示），由于神经语言模型的显著进步，最近成为一个有前景的研究方向。虽然输入上下文限制了可接受输出生成的空间，但与定向生成设置不同，在什么可能接下来出现方面有相当大的自由度。我们的工作解决了神经文本生成在这种增加的自由度下面临的挑战，但我们注意到一些任务，如目标导向对话，可能介于开放式和定向生成之间。

items.en: Given an input text passage as context, the task of open-ended generation is to generate text that forms a coherent continuation from the given context. More formally, given a sequence of m𝑚m tokens x1​…​xmsubscript𝑥1…subscript𝑥𝑚x_{1}\ldots x_{m} as context, the task is to generate the next n𝑛n continuation tokens to obtain the completed sequence x1​…​xm+nsubscript𝑥1…subscript𝑥𝑚𝑛x_{1}\ldots x_{m+n}. We assume that models compute P​(x1:m+n)𝑃subscript𝑥:1𝑚𝑛P(x_{1:m+n}) using the common left-to-right decomposition of the text probability,
items.zh: 给定输入文本段落作为上下文，开放式生成的任务是生成形成从给定上下文连贯延续的文本。更正式地说，给定一个由m个词元x1…xm组成的序列作为上下文，任务是生成接下来的n个延续词元，以获得完整的序列x1…xm+n。我们假设模型使用常见的从左到右分解文本概率来计算P(x1:m+n)，

items.en: which is used to generate the generation token-by-token using a particular decoding strategy.
items.zh: 该分解用于使用特定的解码策略逐词元生成。

items.en: The most commonly used decoding objective, in particular for directed generation, is maximization-based decoding. Assuming that the model assigns higher probability to higher quality text, these decoding strategies search for the continuation with the highest likelihood. Since finding the optimum argmax sequence from recurrent neural language models or Transformers is not tractable (Chen et al., 2018), common practice is to use beam search (Li et al., 2016b; Shen et al., 2017; Wiseman et al., 2017). However, several recent studies on open-ended generation have reported that maximization-based decoding does not lead to high quality text (Fan et al., 2018; Holtzman et al., 2018).
items.zh: 最常用的解码目标，特别是对于定向生成，是基于最大化解码。假设模型将更高的概率分配给更高质量的文本，这些解码策略搜索具有最高似然的延续。由于从循环神经语言模型或Transformer中找到最优的argmax序列是不可行的，常见做法是使用束搜索。然而，最近几项关于开放式生成的研究报告称，基于最大化解码不会产生高质量文本。

items.capEn: Figure 4: The probability of a repeated phrase increases with each repetition, creating a positive feedback loop. We found this effect to hold for the vast majority of phrases we tested, regardless of phrase length or if the phrases were sampled randomly rather than taken from human text.
items.capZh: 图4：重复短语的概率随每次重复而增加，形成正反馈循环。我们发现，这一效应适用于我们测试的绝大多数短语，无论短语长度如何，也无论这些短语是随机采样还是来自人类文本。

items.en: We propose a new stochastic decoding method: Nucleus Sampling. The key idea is to use the shape of the probability distribution to determine the set of tokens to be sampled from. Given a distribution P​(x|x1:i−1)𝑃conditional𝑥subscript𝑥:1𝑖1P(x|x_{1:i-1}), we define its top-p𝑝p vocabulary V(p)⊂Vsuperscript𝑉𝑝𝑉V^{(p)}\subset V as the smallest set such that
items.zh: 我们提出一种新的随机解码方法：Nucleus Sampling（核心采样）。关键思想是利用概率分布的形状来确定要采样的词元集合。给定分布 P(x|x_{1:i-1})，我们将其 top-p 词汇表 V^{(p)} ⊂ V 定义为满足以下条件的最小集合：

items.en: Let p′=∑x∈V(p)P​(x|x1:i−1)superscript𝑝′subscript𝑥superscript𝑉𝑝𝑃conditional𝑥subscript𝑥:1𝑖1p^{\prime}=\sum_{x\in V^{(p)}}P(x|x_{1:i-1}). The original distribution is re-scaled to a new distribution, from which the next word is sampled:
items.zh: 令 p' = ∑_{x∈V^{(p)}} P(x|x_{1:i-1})。原始分布被重新缩放为一个新分布，下一个词将从该分布中采样：

items.en: In practice this means selecting the highest probability tokens whose cumulative probability mass exceeds the pre-chosen threshold p𝑝p. The size of the sampling set will adjust dynamically based on the shape of the probability distribution at each time step. For high values of p𝑝p, this is a small subset of vocabulary that takes up vast majority of the probability mass — the nucleus.
items.zh: 在实践中，这意味着选择累积概率质量超过预设阈值 p 的最高概率词元。采样集的大小将根据每个时间步概率分布的形状动态调整。对于较高的 p 值，这是一个占概率质量绝大部分的词汇子集——即核心（nucleus）。

items.en: Top-k𝑘k sampling has recently become a popular alternative sampling procedure (Fan et al., 2018; Holtzman et al., 2018; Radford et al., 2019). Nucleus Sampling and top-k𝑘k both sample from truncated Neural LM distributions, differing only in the strategy of where to truncate. Choosing where to truncate can be interpreted as determining the generative model’s trustworthy prediction zone.
items.zh: Top-k 采样最近成为一种流行的替代采样程序（Fan 等，2018；Holtzman 等，2018；Radford 等，2019）。Nucleus Sampling 和 top-k 都从截断的神经语言模型分布中采样，仅在截断策略上有所不同。选择截断位置可以解释为确定生成模型的可信预测区域。

items.en: At each time step, the top k𝑘k possible next tokens are sampled from according to their relative probabilities. Formally, given a distribution P​(x|x1:i−1)𝑃conditional𝑥subscript𝑥:1𝑖1P(x|x_{1:i-1}), we define its top-k𝑘k vocabulary V(k)⊂Vsuperscript𝑉𝑘𝑉V^{(k)}\subset V as the set of size k𝑘k which maximizes ∑x∈V(k)P​(x|x1:i−1)subscript𝑥superscript𝑉𝑘𝑃conditional𝑥subscript𝑥:1𝑖1\sum_{x\in V^{(k)}}P(x|x_{1:i-1}). Let p′=∑x∈V(k)P​(x|x1:i−1)superscript𝑝′subscript𝑥superscript𝑉𝑘𝑃conditional𝑥subscript𝑥:1𝑖1p^{\prime}=\sum_{x\in V^{(k)}}P(x|x_{1:i-1}). The distribution is then re-scaled as in equation 3, and sampling is performed based on that distribution. Note that the scaling factor p′superscript𝑝′p^{\prime} can vary wildly at each time-step, in contrast to Nucleus Sampling.
items.zh: 在每个时间步，根据相对概率从 top-k 个可能的下一词元中采样。形式上，给定分布 P(x|x_{1:i-1})，我们将其 top-k 词汇表 V^{(k)} ⊂ V 定义为大小为 k 且最大化 ∑_{x∈V^{(k)}} P(x|x_{1:i-1}) 的集合。令 p' = ∑_{x∈V^{(k)}} P(x|x_{1:i-1})。然后按公式 3 重新缩放分布，并基于该分布进行采样。注意，与 Nucleus Sampling 不同，缩放因子 p' 在每个时间步可能变化很大。

items.capEn: Figure 5: The probability mass assigned to partial human sentences. Flat distributions lead to many moderately probable tokens, while peaked distributions concentrate most probability mass into just a few tokens. The presence of flat distributions makes the use of a small k𝑘k in top-k𝑘k sampling problematic, while the presence of peaked distributions makes large k𝑘k’s problematic.
items.capZh: 图5：分配给部分人类句子的概率质量。平坦分布导致许多中等概率的词元，而尖峰分布将大部分概率质量集中在少数几个词元上。平坦分布的存在使得在 top-k 采样中使用较小的 k 成为问题，而尖峰分布的存在使得较大的 k 成为问题。

items.en: While top-k𝑘k sampling leads to considerably higher quality text than either beam search or sampling from the full distribution, the use of a constant k𝑘k is sub-optimal across varying contexts. As illustrated on the left of Figure 5, in some contexts the head of the next word distribution can be flat across tens or hundreds of reasonable options (e.g. nouns or verbs in generic contexts), while in other contexts most of the probability mass is concentrated in one or a small number of tokens, as on the right of the figure. Therefore if k𝑘k is small, in some contexts there is a risk of generating bland or generic text, while if k𝑘k is large the top-k𝑘k vocabulary will include inappropriate candidates which will have their probability of being sampled increased by the renormalization. Under Nucleus Sampling, the number of candidates considered rises and falls dynamically, corresponding to the changes in the model’s confidence region over the vocabulary which top-k𝑘k sampling fails to capture for any one choice of k𝑘k.
items.zh: 虽然 top-k 采样生成的文本质量远高于束搜索或从完整分布中采样，但在不同上下文中使用固定的 k 是次优的。如图 5 左侧所示，在某些上下文中，下一词分布的头部分可能在数十或数百个合理选项（例如通用上下文中的名词或动词）上是平坦的，而在其他上下文中，大部分概率质量集中在一个或少数几个词元上，如图右侧所示。因此，如果 k 较小，在某些上下文中存在生成平淡或通用文本的风险；而如果 k 较大，top-k 词汇表将包含不合适的候选词，这些候选词通过重新归一化后采样概率会增加。在 Nucleus Sampling 下，考虑的候选词数量会动态上升和下降，对应于模型在词汇表上置信区域的变化，而 top-k 采样对于任何单一的 k 选择都无法捕捉到这种变化。

items.en: Another common approach to sampling-based generation is to shape a probability distribution through temperature (Ackley et al., 1985). Temperature sampling has been applied widely to text generation (Ficler & Goldberg, 2017; Fan et al., 2018; Caccia et al., 2018). Given the logits u1:|V|subscript𝑢:1𝑉u_{1:|V|} and temperature t𝑡t, the softmax is re-estimated as
items.zh: 另一种常见的基于采样的生成方法是通过温度来塑造概率分布（Ackley 等，1985）。温度采样已广泛应用于文本生成（Ficler & Goldberg, 2017；Fan 等，2018；Caccia 等，2018）。给定 logits u_{1:|V|} 和温度 t，softmax 重新估计为：

items.en: Setting t∈[0,1)𝑡01t\in[0,1) skews the distribution towards high probability events, which implicitly lowers the mass in the tail distribution. Low temperature sampling has also been used to partially alleviate the issues of top-k𝑘k sampling discussed above, by shaping the distribution before top-k𝑘k sampling (Radford et al., 2018; Fan et al., 2018). However, recent analysis has shown that, while lowering the temperature improves generation quality, it comes at the cost of decreasing diversity (Caccia et al., 2018; Hashimoto et al., 2019).
items.zh: 设置 t ∈ [0,1) 会使分布偏向高概率事件，从而隐式降低尾部分布的质量。低温采样也被用于部分缓解上述 top-k 采样的问题，方法是在 top-k 采样之前塑造分布（Radford 等，2018；Fan 等，2018）。然而，最近的分析表明，虽然降低温度提高了生成质量，但代价是降低了多样性（Caccia 等，2018；Hashimoto 等，2019）。

items.en: While many neural network architectures have been proposed for language modeling, including LSTMs (Sundermeyer et al., 2012) and convolutional networks (Dauphin et al., 2017), the Transformer architecture (Vaswani et al., 2017) has been the most successful in the extremely large-scale training setups in recent literature (Radford et al., 2018; 2019). In this study we use the Generatively Pre-trained Transformer, version 2 (GPT2; Radford et al., 2019), which was trained on WebText, a 40GB collection of text scraped from the web.333Available at https://github.com/openai/gpt-2-output-dataset We perform experiments using the Large model (762M parameters). Our analysis is based on generating 5,000 text passages, which end upon reaching an end-of-document token or a maximum length of 200 tokens. Texts are generated conditionally, conditioned on the initial paragraph (restricted to 1-40 tokens) of documents in the held-out portion of WebText, except where otherwise mentioned.
items.zh: 尽管已经提出了许多用于语言建模的神经网络架构，包括 LSTM（Sundermeyer 等，2012）和卷积网络（Dauphin 等，2017），但 Transformer 架构（Vaswani 等，2017）在近期文献中的超大规模训练设置中最为成功（Radford 等，2018；2019）。在本研究中，我们使用生成式预训练 Transformer 第二版（GPT2；Radford 等，2019），该模型在 WebText（一个从网络抓取的 40GB 文本集合）上训练。我们使用 Large 模型（762M 参数）进行实验。我们的分析基于生成 5,000 个文本段落，这些段落以遇到文档结束标记或达到最大长度 200 个词元时结束。文本是条件生成的，以 WebText 保留部分中文档的初始段落（限制为 1-40 个词元）为条件，除非另有说明。

items.en: Our first evaluation is to compute the perplexity of generated text using various decoding strategies, according to the model that is being generated from. We compare these perplexities against that of the gold text (Figure 6). Importantly, we argue that the optimal generation strategy should produce text which has a perplexity close to that of the gold text: Even though the model has the ability to generate text that has lower perplexity (higher probability), such text tends to have low diversity and get stuck in repetition loops, as shown in §5 and illustrated in Figure 4.
items.zh: 我们的第一个评估是根据生成模型计算使用不同解码策略生成的文本的困惑度。我们将这些困惑度与黄金文本的困惑度进行比较（图 6）。重要的是，我们认为最优生成策略应产生困惑度接近黄金文本的文本：即使模型有能力生成困惑度更低（概率更高）的文本，这样的文本往往多样性低且容易陷入重复循环，如 §5 所示并在图 4 中说明。

items.en: We see that perplexity of text obtained from pure sampling is worse than the perplexity of the gold. This indicates that the model is confusing itself: sampling too many unlikely tokens and creating context that makes it difficult to recover the human distribution of text, as in Figure 1. Yet, setting the temperature lower creates diversity and repetition issues, as we shall see in §5. Even with our relatively fine-grained parameter sweep, Nucleus Sampling obtains closest perplexity to human text, as shown in Table 1.
items.zh: 我们看到，纯采样得到的文本困惑度比黄金文本的困惑度更差。这表明模型在混淆自身：采样了太多不太可能的词元，并创建了难以恢复人类文本分布的上下文，如图 1 所示。然而，设置较低的温度会产生多样性和重复性问题，我们将在 §5 中看到。即使在我们相对细粒度的参数扫描中，Nucleus Sampling 获得的困惑度最接近人类文本，如表 1 所示。

items.capEn: Figure 6: Perplexities of generations from various decoding methods. Note that beam search has unnaturally low perplexities. A similar effect is seen using a temperature of 0.70.70.7 with top-k𝑘k as in both Radford et al. (2019) and Fan et al. (2018). Sampling, Top-k𝑘k, and Nucleus can all be calibrated to human perplexities, but the first two face coherency issues when their parameters are set this high.
items.capZh: 图6：不同解码方法生成的文本的困惑度。注意，束搜索具有异常低的困惑度。使用温度为 0.7 的 top-k 采样（如 Radford 等（2019）和 Fan 等（2018））也观察到类似效果。采样、Top-k 和 Nucleus 都可以校准到人类困惑度，但前两者在参数设置如此高时会面临连贯性问题。

items.capEn: Figure 7: A rank-frequency plot of the distributional differences between n𝑛n-gram frequencies of human and machine text. Sampling and Nucleus Sampling are by far the closest to the human distribution, while Beam Search clearly follows a very different distribution than natural language.
items.capZh: 图7：人类和机器文本 n-gram 频率分布差异的秩-频率图。采样和 Nucleus Sampling 最接近人类分布，而束搜索显然遵循与自然语言非常不同的分布。

full.sec: 4.3 Natural Language Does Not Maximize Probability
full.secZh: 4.3 自然语言不最大化概率

items.en: One might wonder if the issue with maximization is a search error, i.e., there are higher quality sentences to which the model assigns higher probability than to the decoded ones, beam search has just failed to find them. Yet Figures 2 & 6 show that the per-token probability of natural text is, on average, much lower than text generated by beam search. Natural language rarely remains in a high probability zone for multiple consecutive time steps, instead veering into lower-probability but more informative tokens. Nor does natural language tend to fall into repetition loops, even though the model tends to assign high probability to this, as seen in Figure 4.
items.zh: 人们可能想知道最大化问题是否是搜索错误，即存在模型分配概率高于解码结果的高质量句子，只是束搜索未能找到它们。然而，图 2 和图 6 显示，自然文本的每个词元概率平均远低于束搜索生成的文本。自然语言很少连续多个时间步停留在高概率区域，而是转向低概率但信息量更大的词元。自然语言也不倾向于陷入重复循环，尽管模型倾向于给重复分配高概率，如图 4 所示。

items.en: Why is human-written text not the most probable text? We conjecture that this is an intrinsic property of human language. Language models that assign probabilities one word at a time without a global model of the text will have trouble capturing this effect. Grice’s Maxims of Communication (Grice, 1975) show that people optimize against stating the obvious. Thus, making every word as predictable as possible will be disfavored. This makes solving the problem simply by training larger models or improving neural architectures using standard per-word learning objectives unlikely: such models are forced to favor the lowest common denominator, rather than informative language.
items.zh: 为什么人类撰写的文本不是最可能的文本？我们推测这是人类语言的内在属性。逐词分配概率而没有文本全局模型的语言模型将难以捕捉这一效应。格莱斯交际准则（Grice, 1975）表明，人们会优化以避免陈述显而易见的内容。因此，使每个词尽可能可预测将不受欢迎。这使得仅通过训练更大的模型或使用标准逐词学习目标改进神经架构来解决该问题不太可能：这样的模型被迫偏向最低共同标准，而不是信息性语言。

items.en: In order to compare generations to the reference text, we begin by analyzing their use of vocabulary. Zipf’s law suggests that there is an exponential relationship between the rank of a word and its frequency in text. The Zipfian coefficient s𝑠s can be used to compare the distribution in a given text to a theoretically perfect exponential curve, where s=1𝑠1s=1 (Piantadosi, 2014). Figure 7 shows the vocabulary distributions along with estimated Zipf coefficients for selected parameters of different decoding methods. As expected, pure sampling is the closest to the human distribution, followed by Nucleus Sampling. The visualization of the distribution shows that pure sampling slightly overestimates the use of rare words, likely one reason why pure sampling also has higher perplexity than human text. Furthermore, lower temperature sampling avoids sampling these rare words from the tail, which is why it has been used in some recent work (Fan et al., 2018; Radford et al., 2019).
items.zh: 为了将生成文本与参考文本进行比较，我们首先分析它们的词汇使用。齐普夫定律表明，词的排名与其在文本中的频率之间存在指数关系。齐普夫系数 s 可用于比较给定文本中的分布与理论上完美的指数曲线，其中 s=1（Piantadosi, 2014）。图 7 显示了词汇分布以及不同解码方法选定参数的估计齐普夫系数。正如预期，纯采样最接近人类分布，其次是 Nucleus Sampling。分布的可视化显示，纯采样略微高估了罕见词的使用，这可能是纯采样困惑度高于人类文本的原因之一。此外，低温采样避免了从尾部采样这些罕见词，这就是它在一些近期工作中被使用的原因（Fan 等，2018；Radford 等，2019）。

items.capEn: Figure 8: Self-BLEU calculated on the unconditional generations produced by stochastic decoding methods; lower Self-BLEU scores imply higher diversity. Horizontal blue and orange lines represent human self-BLEU scores. Note how common values of t∈[0.5,1]𝑡0.51t\in[0.5,1] and k∈[1,100]𝑘1100k\in[1,100] result in high self-similarity, whereas “normal” values of p∈[0.9,1)𝑝0.91p\in[0.9,1) closely match the human distribution of text.
items.capZh: 图8：随机解码方法生成的无条件文本的 Self-BLEU 分数；较低的 Self-BLEU 分数意味着更高的多样性。水平蓝色和橙色线代表人类 Self-BLEU 分数。注意，常见的 t ∈ [0.5,1] 和 k ∈ [1,100] 值导致高自相似性，而“正常”的 p ∈ [0.9,1) 值则紧密匹配人类文本分布。

items.en: We follow previous work and compute Self-BLEU (Zhu et al., 2018) as a metric of diversity. Self-BLEU is calculated by computing the BLEU score of each generated document using all other generations in the evaluation set as references. Due to the expense of computing such an operation, we sample 1000 generations, each of which is compared with all 4999 other generations as references. A lower Self-BLEU score implies higher diversity. Figure 8 shows that Self-BLEU results largely follow that of the Zipfian distribution analysis as a diversity measure. It is worth noting that very high values of k𝑘k and t𝑡t are needed to get close to the reference distribution, though these result in unnaturally high perplexity (§4).
items.zh: 我们遵循先前工作，计算 Self-BLEU（Zhu 等，2018）作为多样性指标。Self-BLEU 通过将每个生成文档与评估集中的所有其他生成文档作为参考计算 BLEU 分数来获得。由于计算该操作的开销，我们采样 1000 个生成文本，每个文本与所有其他 4999 个生成文本作为参考进行比较。较低的 Self-BLEU 分数意味着更高的多样性。图 8 显示，Self-BLEU 结果在很大程度上遵循齐普夫分布分析作为多样性度量的结果。值得注意的是，需要非常高的 k 和 t 值才能接近参考分布，尽管这会导致异常高的困惑度（§4）。

items.capEn: Figure 9: We visualize how often different decoding methods get “stuck” in loops within the first 200 tokens. A phrase (minimum length 2) is considered a repetition when it repeats at least three times at the end of the generation. We label points with their parameter values except for t𝑡t and p𝑝p which follow the x-axis. Values of k𝑘k greater than 100 are rarely used in practice and values of p𝑝p are usually in [0.9,1)0.91[0.9,1); therefore Nucleus Sampling is far closer to the human distribution in its usual parameter range. Sampling with temperatures lower than 0.9 severely increase repetition. Finally, although beam search becomes less repetitive according to this metric as beam width increases, this is largely because average length gets shorter as b𝑏b increases (see Appendix A).
items.capZh: 图9：我们可视化了不同解码方法在前200个token中陷入循环的频率。当一个短语（最小长度为2）在生成末尾重复至少三次时，被视为重复。我们标记了参数值对应的点，其中t和p遵循x轴。大于100的k值在实践中很少使用，p值通常在[0.9,1)范围内；因此，在其常用参数范围内，Nucleus Sampling更接近人类分布。温度低于0.9的采样会严重增加重复。最后，尽管随着束宽增加，束搜索在此指标下重复性降低，但这主要是因为平均长度随b增加而变短（见附录A）。

items.en: One attribute of text quality that we can quantify is repetition. Figure 9 shows that Nucleus Sampling and top-k𝑘k sampling have the least repetition for reasonable parameter ranges. Generations from temperature sampling have more repetition unless very high temperatures are used, which we have shown negatively affects coherence (as measured by high perplexity). Further, all stochastic methods face repetition issues when their tuning parameters are set too low, which tends to over-truncate, mimicking greedy search. Therefore we conclude that only Nucleus Sampling satisfies all the distributional criteria for desirable generations.
items.zh: 我们可以量化的文本质量的一个属性是重复性。图9显示，在合理的参数范围内，Nucleus Sampling和top-k采样的重复性最低。温度采样的生成重复性更高，除非使用非常高的温度，但我们已证明这会对连贯性产生负面影响（以高困惑度衡量）。此外，所有随机方法在调参过低时都会面临重复问题，这往往会导致过度截断，模仿贪婪搜索。因此，我们得出结论，只有Nucleus Sampling满足理想生成的所有分布标准。

items.en: Statistical evaluations are unable to measure the coherence of generated text properly. While the metrics in previous sections gave us vital insights into the different decoding methods we compare, human evaluation is still required to get a full measure of the quality of the generated text. However, pure human evaluation does not take into account the diversity of the generated text; therefore we use HUSE (Hashimoto et al., 2019) to combine human and statistical evaluation. HUSE is computed by training a discriminator to distinguish between text drawn from the human and model distributions, based on only two features: The probability assigned by the language model, and human judgements of typicality of generations. Text that is close to the human distribution in terms of quality and diversity should perform well on both likelihood evaluation and human judgements.
items.zh: 统计评估无法正确衡量生成文本的连贯性。虽然前几节的指标为我们提供了所比较的不同解码方法的重要见解，但仍需人工评估才能全面衡量生成文本的质量。然而，纯人工评估没有考虑生成文本的多样性；因此，我们使用HUSE（Hashimoto等人，2019）来结合人工和统计评估。HUSE通过训练一个判别器来计算，该判别器仅基于两个特征区分来自人类分布和模型分布的文本：语言模型分配的概率，以及人类对生成典型性的判断。在质量和多样性方面接近人类分布的文本应在似然评估和人类判断上表现良好。

items.en: As explored in the previous sections, the current best-performing decoding methods rely on truncation of the probability distribution, which yields a probability of 0 for the vast majority of potential tokens. Initial exploration of applying HUSE directly led to top-k𝑘k and Nucleus Sampling receiving scores of nearly 0 due to truncation, despite humans favoring these methods. As a proxy, when generating the text used to compute HUSE, we interpolate (with mass 0.10.10.1) the original probability distribution with the top-k𝑘k and Nucleus Sampling distribution, smoothing the truncated distribution.
items.zh: 如前几节所述，当前性能最佳的解码方法依赖于概率分布的截断，这导致绝大多数潜在token的概率为0。直接应用HUSE的初步探索导致top-k和Nucleus Sampling因截断而得分接近0，尽管人类偏好这些方法。作为替代，在生成用于计算HUSE的文本时，我们将原始概率分布与top-k和Nucleus Sampling分布进行插值（质量为0.1），从而平滑截断分布。

items.en: For each decoding algorithm we annotate 200 generations for typicality, with each generation receiving 20 annotations from 20 different annotators. This results in a total of 4000 annotations per a decoding scheme. We use a KNN classifier to compute HUSE, as in the original paper, with k=13𝑘13k=13 neighbors, which we found led to the higher accuracy in discrimination. The results in Table 1 shows that Nucleus Sampling obtains the highest HUSE score, with Top-k𝑘k sampling performing second best.
items.zh: 对于每种解码算法，我们标注了200个生成的典型性，每个生成由20名不同的标注者进行20次标注。这导致每种解码方案总共4000个标注。我们使用KNN分类器计算HUSE，如原始论文所述，k=13个邻居，我们发现这导致判别准确率更高。表1的结果显示，Nucleus Sampling获得了最高的HUSE分数，Top-k采样表现次之。

items.en: This paper provided a deep analysis into the properties of the most common decoding methods for open-ended language generation. We have shown that likelihood maximizing decoding causes repetition and overly generic language usage, while sampling methods without truncation risk sampling from the low-confidence tail of a model’s predicted distribution. Further, we proposed Nucleus Sampling as a solution that captures the region of confidence of language models effectively. In future work, we wish to dynamically characterize this region of confidence and include a more semantic utility function to guide the decoding process.
items.zh: 本文对开放域语言生成中最常见解码方法的属性进行了深入分析。我们表明，似然最大化解码会导致重复和过于通用的语言使用，而没有截断的采样则可能从模型预测分布的低置信度尾部采样。此外，我们提出了Nucleus Sampling作为一种解决方案，有效捕捉语言模型的置信区域。在未来的工作中，我们希望动态表征这一置信区域，并包含更语义化的效用函数来指导解码过程。

items.en: This research was supported in part by NSF (IIS-1524371), the National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellowship under Grant No. DGE1256082, DARPA CwC through ARO (W911NF15-1- 0543), DARPA MCS program through NIWC Pacific (N66001-19-2-4031), the South African Centre for Artificial Intelligence Research, and the Allen Institute for AI.
items.zh: 本研究部分得到了美国国家科学基金会（IIS-1524371）、国家科学基金会研究生研究奖学金（Grant No. DGE1256082）、DARPA CwC通过ARO（W911NF15-1-0543）、DARPA MCS计划通过NIWC Pacific（N66001-19-2-4031）、南非人工智能研究中心以及艾伦人工智能研究所的支持。
